title: The attention gradient crosses to the four-kernel floor — the transformer's FULL backward pass is now four-way
advances: Completes the transformer block's backward pass on the four-kernel floor. The 3i run (PR #3281) lifted transformer-backprop (127) — the FFN/affine/gelu/softmax+ln gradients — to four-way, but the steepest gradient in the block, backprop THROUGH the softmax-weighted value mixing of attention (dv partition, dalpha, softmax', dq), lived in its own band (transformer-attention-backward) that had only ever run three-way (Go/Rust/TS). Lifting it makes the attention gradient a gated four-kernel invariant on every suite run — so the WHOLE transformer backward (FFN gradient + attention gradient) is now proven on all four kernels, the floor under emitting the trainable block to Metal next to the matvec/backprop kernels already on the M4 GPU.
device: Apple M4 Max (40-core GPU), arm64 Darwin 26.3.1 — proof is host-independent (pure fp64 arithmetic band)

band_lifted.what: the steepest gradient in the transformer: backprop through softmax-weighted value mixing. Forward (single query): s_j = scale*(q.k_j); alpha = softmax(s); o = sum alpha_j v_j. Backward: dv_j = alpha_j*dout (the value-grads PARTITION dout, since sum alpha = 1); dalpha_j = <dout,v_j>; ds = softmax'(alpha,dalpha); dq = scale*sum ds_j*k_j. Proven two ways: the partition law (exact) and by TRAINING the query to steer attention onto a target inside the values' convex hull (loss -> 0 only if dq -> ds -> softmax' -> dalpha all compose). Fixture: keys/values the standard basis (so o = alpha exactly), scale 1, query (0.5,0.5) -> equal attention o=(0.5,0.5); target (0.7,0.3) is the convex combination alpha=(0.7,0.3).
band_lifted.claims: 1: value-grads PARTITION the output-grad (sum dv = dout exactly, both components). 2: score-gradient dq = (-0.2, 0.2), alpha(.)dalpha weighted into the keys (hand-checkable). 4: the unfit attention carries positive loss. 8: ONE step of query training lowers it. 16: it converges — after 50 steps loss rounds to 0, attention steered onto the target. => 31.
band_lifted.preludes: form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/transformer-numerics.fk form-stdlib/transformer-block.fk form-stdlib/transformer-backprop.fk (all already four-way; the tbp-attn-* recipes live in transformer-backprop.fk)

proof: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/transformer-numerics.fk form-stdlib/transformer-block.fk form-stdlib/transformer-backprop.fk form-stdlib/tests/transformer-attention-backward-band.fk  ->  '-> 31', 'fourth arm: 1 band(s) four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables)', '1 ok, 0 divergent'.
verdict: FOUR-WAY (31), 0 divergent — Go, Rust, TypeScript, and the emitted fkwu walker all reproduce the attention backward pass: the value-grad partition law, the score-gradient, and 50-step query training convergence, bit-for-bit at 1e-6.
manifest: registered in form/fourth-arm-bands.txt as 'transformer-attention-backward fks 31', placed in the transformer family directly after 'transformer-backprop fks 127'.
reading: With this band, the transformer block's entire backward pass — the FFN/affine/gelu gradients (transformer-backprop, 127) AND the attention-core gradient (this band, 31) — is four-kernel proven against the convex-hull training law. Combined with the forward numerics (transformer-numerics 4095) and forward block (transformer-block 511), and the load-path dequant math (Q4_K 4095, Q6_K 4095, f16-decode 4095), both the weight load and the block forward+backward now live on the four-way floor. The trainable attention step already runs on the M4 GPU as Metal (scripts/metal_attn_audit.sh, jte-attn-train-msl); this band is the four-kernel-proven algorithm under that emitter. Remaining rungs toward full real-model training/inference on the M4 GPU: the generic typed-layer STACK that assembles many blocks (transformer-block-assembly, still three-way — blocked only by an fkwu capacity limit on the largest composed table, not a divergence), then the generation loop (tokenizer + KV cache + greedy decode).
reproduce: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/transformer-numerics.fk form-stdlib/transformer-block.fk form-stdlib/transformer-backprop.fk form-stdlib/tests/transformer-attention-backward-band.fk — four-way (31), 0 divergent. Run the band individually: rapid back-to-back validate.sh calls share a temp dir and can report a spurious divergence; the canonical suite isolates them.
free_brick_note: Read-the-body again: the recipe (tbp-attn-dv / tbp-attn-dalpha / tbp-attn-dq / tbp-attn-q-train-loss in transformer-backprop.fk) and its reference-pinned band already existed three-way. The brick was one manifest row + confirming fkwu carries the op families (pure fp64 arithmetic + 50-step recursion over d=2 vectors — small tables, well under the assembly band's capacity wall). Design-cost ~nil, proof = seconds, because the body had already built the hard part.
